refs.note: S1 survey — gold set 582 APS papers

refs.title: Topological photonics

refs.title: Network Science
refs.note: Heavy-tail / scale-free motivation for hub suppression; cite for citation graph asymmetry claims

refs.title: Topology of random geometric complexes: a survey
refs.journal: Journal of Applied and Computational Topology
refs.note: K17-RGC live validation survey — 56 gold papers, 100% recall

refs.title: Human social sensing is an untapped resource for computational social science
refs.note: Ge21-HSS live validation survey — 202 gold papers, 100% recall in 2 rounds

refs.title: Graph-Augmented Large Language Model Agents: Current Progress and Future Prospects
refs.note: Le25-GLLM live validation survey — 57 gold papers, 73.7% recall in 1 round

refs.title: Emergence of scaling in random networks
refs.note: Power-law degree distributions in citation networks; cite alongside [5] for heavy-tail claims

refs.title: Networks of scientific papers
refs.note: Early citation network structure analysis

refs.title: SWIFT-Review: a text-mining workbench for systematic review
refs.note: Automated systematic review tool — pre-assembled candidate sets, no graph traversal

refs.title: Toward systematic review automation: a practical guide to using machine learning tools in research synthesis
refs.note: ML-based screening for systematic review automation

refs.title: An O(m) algorithm for cores decomposition of networks
refs.note: k-core decomposition; hub removal for graph compression

refs.title: Subnets of scale-free networks are not scale-free: sampling properties of networks
refs.note: Degree-constrained sampling; related to Pareto filter design

refs.title: CiteSpace II: Detecting and visualizing emerging trends and transient patterns in scientific literature
refs.journal: Journal of the American Society for Information Science and Technology
refs.note: Citation-expansion from large anchor sets; contrast with LitDiscover minimal-seed framing

refs.title: Algebraic Vertex Ordering of a Sparse Graph for Adjacency Access Locality and Graph Compression
refs.booktitle: 2024 IEEE High Performance Extreme Computing Conference (HPEC)
refs.note: Hub-node special handling during graph traversal — justifies Pareto filter as principled, not heuristic

refs.title: Elicit: The AI Research Assistant
refs.note: LLM-assisted literature search tool — query-driven, no formal yield-based stopping criterion. Cite in §2 Related Work alongside ResearchRabbit.
refs.bib_entry: @software{elicit, author = {{Elicit}}, title = {Elicit: The AI Research Assistant}, url = {https://elicit.com}, year = {2024}, note = {Accessed: 2026-04-11} }

refs.title: ResearchRabbit
refs.note: Citation discovery tool — graph-based exploration from anchor papers, but starts from large anchor sets not minimal seeds. Cite in §2 Related Work.

refs.title: Connected Papers | Find and explore academic papers
refs.note: Graph-based literature exploration from curated bibliographies — no formal publication. Contrast with LitDiscover's minimal-seed framing in §2 Related Work.
refs.bib_entry: Connected Papers. (n.d.). Connected Papers | Find and explore academic papers. Retrieved 2026-04-11, from https://www.connectedpapers.com/

refs.title: Modelling citation networks
refs.note: ~80% of citations arise from bibliographic copying of references-of-references (local search); ~20% from global search. Directly corroborates backward traversal priority in LitDiscover. Cite in §3 (Architecture) or §5 (Structural Motivation).

refs.title: Leveraging LLM-based agents for social science research: insights from citation network simulations
refs.note: CiteAgent: LLM agents simulate citation network formation. Key finding: recommendation algorithm (not citation bias) drives preferential attachment — suppressing high-citation paper visibility reduces hub formation. Directly supports Pareto filter rationale. Cite in §2 (citation-graph analysis area) and §5 (hub formation mechanism).

refs.title: Guidelines for snowballing in systematic literature studies and a replication in software engineering
refs.booktitle: International Conference on Evaluation and Assessment in Software Engineering (EASE)
refs.note: Defines snowballing methodology: backward (following references) and forward (following citations) chaining from seed papers. LitDiscover automates exactly this process. Cite in §2 Related Work to establish that we are formalising and automating a known manual methodology.

refs.title: An open source machine learning framework for efficient and transparent systematic reviews
refs.note: ASReview: active learning framework for systematic review screening. Key contrast with LitDiscover: ASReview assumes a pre-assembled candidate set and prioritises screening order; LitDiscover discovers the candidate set via citation traversal before screening begins. Cite in §2 (automated systematic review area) alongside SWIFT-Review [11] and RobotSearch [12].

_zotero_note: User's Zotero library is at /Users/davidgoh/Downloads/refs.bib — general library, does NOT contain LitDiscover-specific entries. Elicit/ResearchRabbit/ConnectedPapers were added manually above. To export a focused .bib: in Zotero, select only the LitDiscover collection → right-click → Export Collection → Better BibTeX.
_latex_note: To convert to LaTeX: pandoc -s paper.md -o paper.tex --citeproc --bibliography=refs.bib --csl=your-style.csl. Citation keys in this file (e.g. Barabasi2016, Floros2024, Elicit2024) are ready to use as \cite{} keys once .bib is generated.
_venue_note: Venue not yet decided. Top candidates: JCDL 2026 (best topical fit — digital libraries + IR), JASIST journal (systematic review automation, longer cycle), EMNLP Findings 2026 (LLM angle, broader audience), CIKM 2026 (IR+graphs). Recommend arXiv preprint first, then target JCDL or JASIST.
